question: Ask the user to enter their full name (which may include spaces). Read the entire line into an `std::string`. Then, print the full name back to the user and also display the total number of characters in the name.

test_cases.expected:
Please enter your full name: Full Name: John Doe
Number of characters: 24

Please enter your full name: Full Name: Alice Wonderland
Number of characters: 24

Please enter your full name: Full Name: A
Number of characters: 24

errors: The program uses `sizeof(fullName)` to determine the number of characters. `sizeof` returns the size in bytes of the `std::string` object itself (e.g., 24 or 32 bytes on common systems), not the number of characters currently stored within the string. This is a semantic misinterpretation of how to get the length of an `std::string`. The correct way is to use `fullName.length()` or `fullName.size()`.
